prompt: The Externalizable class reads fields in the wrong order. What is the bug?
choices: Externalizable always reads fields in alphabetical order
readExternal must read fields in the same order they were written in writeExternal; reading b first when a was written first swaps the values
writeExternal and readExternal must write/read all fields using writeObject/readObject
int fields must be written as bytes in Externalizable
explanation: Externalizable streams are positional — data must be read in exactly the same order it was written. writeExternal writes a then b; readExternal reads into b first (getting a's value) then a (getting b's value). Fix: read in the same order: a = in.readInt(); b = in.readInt();
